Acceptance Test Specification 6: 7: Version: 1.0 8: 9: --- 10: 11: # Purpose 12: 13: This document defines the minimum acceptance criteria for the platform. 14: 15: A feature is not complete unless all associated acceptance tests pass. 16: 17: The implementation must satisfy every test below. 18: 19: --- 20: 21: # SECTION 1: Authentication 22: 23: --- 24: 25: ## Final Acceptance Criteria 1100: 1101: The project is considered complete only if: 1102: 1103: * All acceptance tests pass 1104: * No critical bugs remain 1105: * All PRD requirements implemented 1106: * All TDD requirements implemented 1107: * Security requirements implemented 1108: * Production deployment successful 1109: * Leaderboard calculations verified 1110: * Mobile experience verified 1111: * Admin workflows verified 1112: 1113: Completion Requirement: 1114: 1115: 100% PASS 1: # Build Rules 2: 3: The application must be production-ready. 4: 5: Do NOT generate placeholders. 6: 7: Do NOT generate mock implementations. 8: 9: Do NOT generate TODO comments. 10: 11: Do NOT skip functionality defined in the PRD. 12: 13: Do NOT create fake APIs. 14: 15: Do NOT create fake data sources. 16: 17: All features must be implemented fully. 18: 19: Requirements: 20: 21: * Strict TypeScript 22: * No `any` 23: * Zod validation everywhere 24: * React Hook Form 25: * Server Actions preferred 26: * Proper error handling 27: * Loading states 28: * Empty states 29: * Mobile responsiveness 30: * Accessibility support 31: 32: Authentication: 33: 34: * Supabase Auth 35: * Email/password login 36: * Protected routes 37: 38: Database: 39: 40: * PostgreSQL 41: * Supabase migrations 42: * Proper indexes 43: * RLS policies 44: 45: UI: 46: 47: * shadcn/ui only 48: * TailwindCSS 49: * Dark theme 50: * Football-inspired design 51: 52: Performance: 53: 54: * Avoid unnecessary re-renders 55: * Use caching where appropriate 56: 57: Code Quality: 58: 59: * Modular architecture 60: * Reusable components 61: * Typed APIs 62: * Clean folder structure 63: 64: Testing: 65: 66: * Unit tests for scoring engine 67: * Integration tests for uploads 68: * E2E tests for submission workflow 69: 70: Deployment Target: 71: 72: * Vercel 73: * Production-ready 1: # DATABASE_SPEC.md 2: 3: # FIFA World Cup 2026 ML Prediction Challenge 4: 5: ## Database Specification 6: 7: Version: 1.0 8: 9: --- 10: 11: # Database Engine 12: 13: PostgreSQL (Supabase) 14: 15: Requirements: 16: 17: * UUID primary keys 18: * Foreign key constraints 19: * Row Level Security enabled 20: * Proper indexing 21: * Audit logging 22: * Soft delete where appropriate 23: 24: --- 25: 26: # ENUMS 27: 28: ## UserRole 29: 30: ```sql 31: participant 32: admin 33: ``` 34: 35: ## MatchStatus 36: 37: ```sql 38: scheduled 39: live 40: completed 41: cancelled 42: ``` 43: 44: ## TournamentStage 45: 46: ```sql 47: group_stage 48: round_of_32 49: round_of_16 50: quarter_final 51: semi_final 52: third_place 53: final 54: ``` 55: 56: --- 57: 58: # TABLE: profiles 59: 60: Purpose: 61: 62: Stores application user metadata linked to Supabase Auth. 63: 64: Columns: 65: 66: ```sql 67: id UUID PRIMARY KEY 68: ``` 69: 70: References: 71: 72: ```sql 73: auth.users(id) 74: ``` 75: 76: Additional Columns: 77: 78: ```sql 79: email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE 80: 81: role UserRole NOT NULL DEFAULT 'participant' 82: 83: is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE 84: 85: created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 86: 87: updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 88: ``` 89: 90: Indexes: 91: 92: ```sql 93: email 94: role 95: ``` 96: 97: --- 98: 99: # TABLE: teams 100: 101: Purpose: 102: 103: Competition team identity. 104: 105: Each authenticated user owns one team. 106: 107: Columns: 108: 109: ```sql 110: id UUID PRIMARY KEY 111: 112: owner_id UUID NOT NULL 113: 114: team_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE 115: 116: submission_locked BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE 117: 118: created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 119: 120: updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 121: ``` 122: 123: Foreign Keys: 124: 125: ```sql 126: owner_id -> profiles.id 127: ``` 128: 129: Indexes: 130: 131: ```sql 132: team_name 133: owner_id 134: ``` 135: 136: Constraints: 137: 138: ```sql 139: one user owns one team 140: ``` 141: 142: --- 143: 144: # TABLE: matches 145: 146: Purpose: 147: 148: Stores FIFA World Cup matches. 149: 150: Columns: 151: 152: ```sql 153: id UUID PRIMARY KEY 154: 155: match_code TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE 156: 157: stage TournamentStage NOT NULL 158: 159: home_team TEXT NOT NULL 160: 161: away_team TEXT NOT NULL 162: 163: kickoff_time TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 164: 165: status MatchStatus NOT NULL 166: 167: multiplier NUMERIC(4,2) NOT NULL 168: 169: created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 170: 171: updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 172: ``` 173: 174: Indexes: 175: 176: ```sql 177: match_code 178: 179: stage 180: 181: status 182: 183: kickoff_time 184: ``` 185: 186: Default Multipliers: 187: 188: ```sql 189: group_stage = 1.0 190: 191: round_of_32 = 1.2 192: 193: round_of_16 = 1.5 194: 195: quarter_final = 2.0 196: 197: semi_final = 3.0 198: 199: third_place = 2.5 200: 201: final = 5.0 202: ``` 203: 204: --- 205: 206: # TABLE: submissions 207: 208: Purpose: 209: 210: Stores uploaded prediction file metadata. 211: 212: Columns: 213: 214: ```sql 215: id UUID PRIMARY KEY 216: 217: team_id UUID NOT NULL 218: 219: file_path TEXT NOT NULL 220: 221: file_name TEXT NOT NULL 222: 223: uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 224: 225: is_valid BOOLEAN NOT NULL 226: 227: validation_errors JSONB 228: 229: locked_at TIMESTAMPTZ 230: ``` 231: 232: Foreign Keys: 233: 234: ```sql 235: team_id -> teams.id 236: ``` 237: 238: Constraints: 239: 240: ```sql 241: one submission per team 242: ``` 243: 244: Unique: 245: 246: ```sql 247: team_id 248: ``` 249: 250: --- 251: 252: # TABLE: predictions 253: 254: Purpose: 255: 256: Stores prediction data for every match. 257: 258: Columns: 259: 260: ```sql 261: id UUID PRIMARY KEY 262: 263: team_id UUID NOT NULL 264: 265: match_id UUID NOT NULL 266: 267: winner TEXT 268: 269: home_score INTEGER 270: 271: away_score INTEGER 272: 273: extra_time_home INTEGER 274: 275: extra_time_away INTEGER 276: 277: penalty_home INTEGER 278: 279: penalty_away INTEGER 280: 281: goal_scorers JSONB 282: 283: first_goal_scorer TEXT 284: 285: possession_home NUMERIC(5,2) 286: 287: possession_away NUMERIC(5,2) 288: 289: shots_home INTEGER 290: 291: shots_away INTEGER 292: 293: xg_home NUMERIC(5,2) 294: 295: xg_away NUMERIC(5,2) 296: 297: yellow_home INTEGER 298: 299: yellow_away INTEGER 300: 301: red_home INTEGER 302: 303: red_away INTEGER 304: 305: confidence INTEGER 306: 307: created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 308: ``` 309: 310: Foreign Keys: 311: 312: ```sql 313: team_id -> teams.id 314: 315: match_id -> matches.id 316: ``` 317: 318: Unique: 319: 320: ```sql 321: (team_id, match_id) 322: ``` 323: 324: Indexes: 325: 326: ```sql 327: team_id 328: 329: match_id 330: ``` 331: 332: --- 333: 334: # TABLE: champion_predictions 335: 336: Purpose: 337: 338: Stores predicted tournament winner. 339: 340: Columns: 341: 342: ```sql 343: id UUID PRIMARY KEY 344: 345: team_id UUID NOT NULL UNIQUE 346: 347: champion TEXT NOT NULL 348: 349: created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 350: ``` 351: 352: Foreign Keys: 353: 354: ```sql 355: team_id -> teams.id 356: ``` 357: 358: --- 359: 360: # TABLE: actual_results 361: 362: Purpose: 363: 364: Official results entered by admins. 365: 366: Columns: 367: 368: ```sql 369: id UUID PRIMARY KEY 370: 371: match_id UUID NOT NULL UNIQUE 372: 373: winner TEXT 374: 375: home_score INTEGER 376: 377: away_score INTEGER 378: 379: extra_time_home INTEGER 380: 381: extra_time_away INTEGER 382: 383: penalty_home INTEGER 384: 385: penalty_away INTEGER 386: 387: goal_scorers JSONB 388: 389: first_goal_scorer TEXT 390: 391: possession_home NUMERIC(5,2) 392: 393: possession_away NUMERIC(5,2) 394: 395: shots_home INTEGER 396: 397: shots_away INTEGER 398: 399: xg_home NUMERIC(5,2) 400: 401: xg_away NUMERIC(5,2) 402: 403: yellow_home INTEGER 404: 405: yellow_away INTEGER 406: 407: red_home INTEGER 408: 409: red_away INTEGER 410: 411: updated_by UUID 412: 413: updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 414: ``` 415: 416: Foreign Keys: 417: 418: ```sql 419: match_id -> matches.id 420: 421: updated_by -> profiles.id 422: ``` 423: 424: Indexes: 425: 426: ```sql 427: match_id 428: ``` 429: 430: --- 431: 432: # TABLE: scoring_rules 433: 434: Purpose: 435: 436: Admin configurable scoring system. 437: 438: Columns: 439: 440: ```sql 441: id UUID PRIMARY KEY 442: 443: rule_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE 444: 445: rule_name TEXT NOT NULL 446: 447: points INTEGER NOT NULL 448: 449: is_enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE 450: 451: updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 452: ``` 453: 454: Default Rules: 455: 456: ```sql 457: correct_winner = 20 458: 459: correct_draw = 20 460: 461: exact_scoreline = 40 462: 463: correct_goal_difference = 15 464: 465: one_team_score_correct = 10 466: 467: correct_scorer = 10 468: 469: correct_goal_count = 10 470: 471: exact_scorer_list = 25 472: 473: possession_accuracy = 10 474: 475: shots_accuracy = 10 476: 477: xg_accuracy = 10 478: 479: yellow_cards_accuracy = 5 480: 481: red_cards_exact = 10 482: 483: penalty_winner = 20 484: 485: penalty_score = 30 486: 487: confidence_bonus = 10 488: 489: confidence_penalty = -10 490: 491: champion_prediction = 250 492: ``` 493: 494: --- 495: 496: # TABLE: leaderboard 497: 498: Purpose: 499: 500: Pre-calculated rankings. 501: 502: Columns: 503: 504: ```sql 505: id UUID PRIMARY KEY 506: 507: team_id UUID NOT NULL UNIQUE 508: 509: rank INTEGER 510: 511: total_score NUMERIC(10,2) 512: 513: accuracy_percentage NUMERIC(5,2) 514: 515: winner_score NUMERIC(10,2) 516: 517: scoreline_score NUMERIC(10,2) 518: 519: scorer_score NUMERIC(10,2) 520: 521: stats_score NUMERIC(10,2) 522: 523: champion_score NUMERIC(10,2) 524: 525: confidence_score NUMERIC(10,2) 526: 527: updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 528: ``` 529: 530: Foreign Keys: 531: 532: ```sql 533: team_id -> teams.id 534: ``` 535: 536: Indexes: 537: 538: ```sql 539: rank 540: 541: total_score DESC 542: ``` 543: 544: --- 545: 546: # TABLE: analytics_cache 547: 548: Purpose: 549: 550: Stores precomputed analytics. 551: 552: Columns: 553: 554: ```sql 555: id UUID PRIMARY KEY 556: 557: metric_key TEXT UNIQUE 558: 559: metric_value JSONB 560: 561: updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ 562: ``` 563: 564: --- 565: 566: # TABLE: audit_logs 567: 568: Purpose: 569: 570: Tracks administrative actions. 571: 572: Columns: 573: 574: ```sql 575: id UUID PRIMARY KEY 576: 577: actor_id UUID 578: 579: action TEXT 580: 581: entity_type TEXT 582: 583: entity_id UUID 584: 585: payload JSONB 586: 587: created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL 588: ``` 589: 590: Indexes: 591: 592: ```sql 593: actor_id 594: 595: entity_type 596: 597: created_at 598: ``` 599: 600: --- 601: 602: # ROW LEVEL SECURITY 603: 604: ## profiles 605: 606: Participant: 607: 608: Read own record. 609: 610: Admin: 611: 612: Read all. 613: 614: --- 615: 616: ## teams 617: 618: Participant: 619: 620: Read own team. 621: 622: Admin: 623: 624: Read all. 625: 626: --- 627: 628: ## predictions 629: 630: Participant: 631: 632: Read own predictions. 633: 634: Admin: 635: 636: Read all predictions. 637: 638: --- 639: 640: ## submissions 641: 642: Participant: 643: 644: Read own submission. 645: 646: Admin: 647: 648: Read all submissions. 649: 650: --- 651: 652: ## actual_results 653: 654: Public: 655: 656: Read. 657: 658: Admin: 659: 660: Create / Update. 661: 662: --- 663: 664: ## leaderboard 665: 666: Public: 667: 668: Read. 669: 670: Admin: 671: 672: Read. 673: 674: --- 675: 676: ## analytics_cache 677: 678: Public: 679: 680: Read. 681: 682: Admin: 683: 684: Update. 685: 686: --- 687: 688: ## scoring_rules 689: 690: Public: 691: 692: Read. 693: 694: Admin: 695: 696: Update. 697: 698: --- 699: 700: # STORAGE 701: 702: Bucket: 703: 704: prediction-files 705: 706: Structure: 707: 708: ```text 709: prediction-files/ 710: team-id/ 711: predictions.csv 712: ``` 713: 714: Permissions: 715: 716: Participant: 717: 718: Upload own file. 719: 720: Admin: 721: 722: Read all files. 723: 724: Public: 725: 726: No access. 727: 728: --- 729: 730: # DATABASE INDEXING REQUIREMENTS 731: 732: Create indexes for: 733: 734: ```sql 735: leaderboard.total_score 736: 737: matches.stage 738: 739: matches.status 740: 741: predictions.team_id 742: 743: predictions.match_id 744: 745: actual_results.match_id 746: 747: audit_logs.created_at 748: ``` 749: 750: --- 751: 752: # DATABASE DESIGN RULES 753: 754: Do not modify schema without migration. 755: 756: Do not store derived scores in prediction rows. 757: 758: Leaderboard is generated. 759: 760: Analytics are cached. 761: 762: All primary keys must use UUID. 763: 764: All timestamps must use TIMESTAMPTZ. 765: 766: Enable Row Level Security on every table. 767: 768: Use cascading deletes only where explicitly required. 1: Phase 1 2: 3: Project Setup 4: 5: Phase 2 6: 7: Supabase Integration 8: 9: Phase 3 10: 11: Authentication 12: 13: Phase 4 14: 15: Database Schema 16: 17: Phase 5 18: 19: CSV Upload 20: 21: Phase 6 22: 23: Validation Engine 24: 25: Phase 7 26: 27: Scoring Engine 28: 29: Phase 8 30: 31: Leaderboard 32: 33: Phase 9 34: 35: Analytics 36: 37: Phase 10 38: 39: Admin Dashboard 40: 41: Phase 11 42: 43: Testing 44: 45: Phase 12 46: 47: Deployment 1: # FIFA World Cup 2026 ML Prediction Challenge Platform 2: 3: ## Enterprise Product Requirements Document (PRD) 4: 5: Version: 1.0 6: 7: Project Name: FIFA World Cup 2026 ML Prediction Challenge 8: 9: Organization: MuLearn Hackathon 10: 11: Target Launch: Before FIFA World Cup 2026 12: 13: --- 14: 15: # 1. Executive Summary 16: 17: ## Objective 18: 19: Build a web-based Machine Learning evaluation platform for the FIFA World Cup 2026. 20: 21: Participants develop predictive models and submit a single prediction file before the tournament begins. 22: 23: The platform evaluates predictions against actual tournament results and automatically ranks participants on a public leaderboard. 24: 25: The platform DOES NOT run ML models. 26: 27: The platform only: 28: 29: * Accepts prediction outputs 30: * Validates submissions 31: * Stores predictions 32: * Compares predictions with actual results 33: * Calculates scores 34: * Displays rankings and analytics 35: 36: --- 37: 38: # 2. Product Vision 39: 40: Create a Kaggle-style evaluation platform specifically designed for FIFA World Cup predictions. 41: 42: The platform should: 43: 44: * Reward prediction accuracy 45: * Support complex football predictions 46: * Encourage model explainability 47: * Provide transparent scoring 48: * Create competitive engagement throughout the tournament 49: 50: --- 51: 52: # 3. Success Metrics 53: 54: ## Primary 55: 56: * Successful submission rate >95% 57: * Zero scoring disputes caused by system errors 58: * Real-time leaderboard updates after every completed match 59: 60: ## Secondary 61: 62: * Leaderboard engagement 63: * Prediction analytics usage 64: * Tournament completion rate 65: 66: --- 67: 68: # 4. User Roles 69: 70: ## Participant 71: 72: Can: 73: 74: * Register 75: * Login 76: * Download prediction template 77: * Upload prediction CSV 78: * View personal score breakdown 79: * View public leaderboard 80: * View analytics 81: 82: Cannot: 83: 84: * Edit submissions after lock 85: * View other participants' predictions 86: * Access admin tools 87: 88: --- 89: 90: ## Admin 91: 92: Can: 93: 94: * Manage tournament data 95: * Create matches 96: * Update actual results 97: * Manage users 98: * Manage scoring configuration 99: * Recalculate leaderboard 100: * View all submissions 101: * Lock/unlock submissions 102: 103: --- 104: 105: ## Public Visitor 106: 107: Can: 108: 109: * View leaderboard 110: * View tournament analytics 111: * View rankings 112: 113: Cannot: 114: 115: * Submit predictions 116: * View participant prediction files 117: 118: --- 119: 120: # 5. Tournament Scope 121: 122: Competition covers the entire FIFA World Cup 2026. 123: 124: Prediction scope: 125: 126: * Group Stage 127: * Round of 32 128: * Round of 16 129: * Quarter Finals 130: * Semi Finals 131: * Third Place Match 132: * Final 133: 134: All tournament matches must be predicted. 135: 136: --- 137: 138: # 6. Submission Workflow 139: 140: ## Submission Model 141: 142: Single Submission Only 143: 144: Participants upload one CSV containing predictions for the entire tournament. 145: 146: Once accepted: 147: 148: * Submission becomes immutable 149: * No edits allowed 150: * No replacement uploads 151: 152: Submission deadline: 153: 154: Before the first Group Stage match starts. 155: 156: --- 157: 158: # 7. Prediction Data Requirements 159: 160: Each match prediction must include: 161: 162: ## Match Result 163: 164: * Predicted winner 165: * Predicted final score 166: * Predicted extra time score 167: * Predicted penalty shootout score (if applicable) 168: 169: --- 170: 171: ## Goal Scoring 172: 173: * Exact goal scorers 174: * Goal count per scorer 175: * First goal scorer 176: 177: --- 178: 179: ## Match Statistics 180: 181: * Possession % 182: * Shots on target 183: * Expected Goals (xG) 184: * Yellow cards 185: * Red cards 186: 187: --- 188: 189: ## Confidence 190: 191: * Winner confidence score (0-100) 192: 193: Example: 194: 195: Brazil win probability = 87% 196: 197: --- 198: 199: ## Tournament Prediction 200: 201: * Predicted tournament champion 202: 203: --- 204: 205: # 8. CSV Specification 206: 207: ## Required Columns 208: 209: match_id 210: 211: winner 212: 213: home_score 214: 215: away_score 216: 217: extra_time_home 218: 219: extra_time_away 220: 221: penalty_home 222: 223: penalty_away 224: 225: goal_scorers 226: 227: goal_counts 228: 229: first_goal_scorer 230: 231: possession_home 232: 233: possession_away 234: 235: shots_on_target_home 236: 237: shots_on_target_away 238: 239: xg_home 240: 241: xg_away 242: 243: yellow_cards_home 244: 245: yellow_cards_away 246: 247: red_cards_home 248: 249: red_cards_away 250: 251: confidence 252: 253: --- 254: 255: ## Goal Scorer Format 256: 257: Mbappe:2;Dembele:1 258: 259: --- 260: 261: ## Example Row 262: 263: R32_001 264: 265: Brazil 266: 267: 2 268: 269: 1 270: 271: 0 272: 273: 0 274: 275: 0 276: 277: 0 278: 279: Vinicius:1;Rodrygo:1 280: 281: Vinicius 282: 283: 58 284: 285: 42 286: 287: 7 288: 289: 4 290: 291: 1.8 292: 293: 0.9 294: 295: 2 296: 297: 3 298: 299: 0 300: 301: 0 302: 303: 87 304: 305: --- 306: 307: # 9. Validation Rules 308: 309: Submission validation must verify: 310: 311: * Required fields exist 312: * Match IDs valid 313: * Team names valid 314: * Player names valid 315: * Scores non-negative 316: * Possession totals = 100 317: * Confidence range 0-100 318: * Duplicate matches absent 319: * Champion exists in tournament 320: 321: Invalid submissions are rejected. 322: 323: --- 324: 325: # 10. Name Normalization 326: 327: System must normalize: 328: 329: Examples: 330: 331: Mbappe 332: 333: Kylian Mbappe 334: 335: K. Mbappe 336: 337: Should map to same player. 338: 339: Use fuzzy matching with configurable threshold. 340: 341: --- 342: 343: # 11. Actual Result Management 344: 345: Admin manually enters: 346: 347: * Winner 348: * Score 349: * Extra time 350: * Penalties 351: * Goal scorers 352: * Match statistics 353: 354: Admin remains source of truth. 355: 356: No dependency on external sports APIs. 357: 358: Optional future enhancement: 359: 360: Automatic API result import. 361: 362: --- 363: 364: # 12. Scoring Engine 365: 366: Scoring system must be configurable. 367: 368: All values stored in database. 369: 370: Admin can modify without code deployment. 371: 372: --- 373: 374: # 13. Default Scoring Configuration 375: 376: ## Match Outcome 377: 378: Correct Winner = 20 379: 380: Correct Draw = 20 381: 382: Exact Scoreline = 40 383: 384: Correct Goal Difference = 15 385: 386: One Team Goal Count Correct = 10 387: 388: --- 389: 390: ## Goal Scorers 391: 392: Correct Scorer = 10 393: 394: Correct Goal Count By Scorer = 10 395: 396: Exact Scorer List = 25 397: 398: --- 399: 400: ## Match Statistics 401: 402: Possession Within ±5% = 10 403: 404: Shots On Target Within ±2 = 10 405: 406: xG Within ±0.5 = 10 407: 408: Yellow Cards Within ±1 = 5 409: 410: Red Cards Exact = 10 411: 412: --- 413: 414: ## Penalties 415: 416: Correct Shootout Winner = 20 417: 418: Exact Shootout Score = 30 419: 420: --- 421: 422: ## Confidence 423: 424: Correct Prediction Above 80% Confidence = +10 425: 426: Incorrect Prediction Above 80% Confidence = -10 427: 428: --- 429: 430: ## Champion 431: 432: Correct Champion = 250 433: 434: --- 435: 436: # 14. Stage Multipliers 437: 438: Group Stage = 1.0x 439: 440: Round of 32 = 1.2x 441: 442: Round of 16 = 1.5x 443: 444: Quarter Final = 2.0x 445: 446: Semi Final = 3.0x 447: 448: Third Place = 2.5x 449: 450: Final = 5.0x 451: 452: --- 453: 454: # 15. Accuracy Percentage 455: 456: Formula: 457: 458: Accuracy % 459: 460: = 461: 462: Earned Points 463: 464: / 465: 466: Maximum Possible Points 467: 468: × 469: 470: 100 471: 472: Display: 473: 474: 74.3% 475: 476: --- 477: 478: # 16. Leaderboard 479: 480: Publicly accessible. 481: 482: Display: 483: 484: * Rank 485: * Team Name 486: * Total Points 487: * Accuracy % 488: * Correct Winner % 489: * Correct Score % 490: * Champion Status 491: 492: Updates after every completed match. 493: 494: --- 495: 496: # 17. User Score Breakdown 497: 498: Participant dashboard must show: 499: 500: Winner Accuracy 501: 502: Score Accuracy 503: 504: Scorer Accuracy 505: 506: Statistics Accuracy 507: 508: Champion Prediction Status 509: 510: Confidence Score Impact 511: 512: Total Score 513: 514: --- 515: 516: # 18. Analytics Dashboard 517: 518: ## Tournament Analytics 519: 520: Display: 521: 522: * Overall prediction accuracy 523: * Most predictable match 524: * Least predictable match 525: * Best performing participant 526: * Average confidence score 527: 528: --- 529: 530: ## Comparison Analytics 531: 532: Participant A vs Participant B 533: 534: Compare: 535: 536: * Winner accuracy 537: * Score accuracy 538: * Scorer accuracy 539: * Confidence calibration 540: 541: --- 542: 543: # 19. Admin Dashboard 544: 545: ## User Management 546: 547: * View participants 548: * Disable accounts 549: * Export data 550: 551: --- 552: 553: ## Tournament Management 554: 555: * Create matches 556: * Update matches 557: * Mark completed matches 558: 559: --- 560: 561: ## Results Management 562: 563: * Enter actual results 564: * Edit actual results 565: * Trigger recalculation 566: 567: --- 568: 569: ## Scoring Management 570: 571: * Modify scoring rules 572: * Modify multipliers 573: * Recalculate leaderboard 574: 575: --- 576: 577: # 20. Authentication 578: 579: Method: 580: 581: Email + Password 582: 583: Powered by Supabase Auth. 584: 585: Features: 586: 587: * Registration 588: * Login 589: * Password reset 590: * Session management 591: 592: --- 593: 594: # 21. Database Design 595: 596: Core Tables 597: 598: users 599: 600: teams 601: 602: submissions 603: 604: matches 605: 606: predictions 607: 608: actual_results 609: 610: scoring_rules 611: 612: leaderboard 613: 614: analytics_cache 615: 616: audit_logs 617: 618: --- 619: 620: # 22. Security 621: 622: Row Level Security enabled. 623: 624: Participants may access only: 625: 626: * Their profile 627: * Their submission 628: 629: Admins may access all records. 630: 631: Leaderboard remains public. 632: 633: --- 634: 635: # 23. UI / UX Requirements 636: 637: Design inspired by mufifa.mulearn.org 638: 639: Requirements: 640: 641: * Dark theme 642: * Neon football aesthetic 643: * Modern tournament visuals 644: * Responsive layouts 645: * Mobile-first design 646: * High contrast accessibility 647: 648: --- 649: 650: # 24. Pages 651: 652: Home 653: 654: Leaderboard 655: 656: Analytics 657: 658: Login 659: 660: Register 661: 662: Submission Portal 663: 664: My Dashboard 665: 666: Admin Dashboard 667: 668: Scoring Settings 669: 670: Tournament Management 671: 672: Results Entry 673: 674: --- 675: 676: # 25. Performance Targets 677: 678: Participants: 679: 680: <500 681: 682: Leaderboard Load: 683: 684: <2 seconds 685: 686: CSV Validation: 687: 688: <5 seconds 689: 690: Score Recalculation: 691: 692: <30 seconds 693: 694: --- 695: 696: # 26. Technology Stack 697: 698: Frontend 699: 700: Next.js 15 App Router 701: 702: TypeScript 703: 704: Tailwind CSS 705: 706: shadcn/ui 707: 708: Recharts 709: 710: TanStack Table 711: 712: React Hook Form 713: 714: Zod 715: 716: --- 717: 718: Backend 719: 720: Supabase 721: 722: PostgreSQL 723: 724: Supabase Auth 725: 726: Supabase Storage 727: 728: Row Level Security 729: 730: --- 731: 732: Deployment 733: 734: Vercel 735: 736: --- 737: 738: # 27. Acceptance Criteria 739: 740: Participant can upload valid CSV. 741: 742: Invalid CSV rejected. 743: 744: Submission becomes immutable. 745: 746: Admin can enter results. 747: 748: Scores automatically update. 749: 750: Leaderboard updates correctly. 751: 752: Analytics update correctly. 753: 754: Authentication works. 755: 756: Mobile experience fully functional. 757: 758: Public leaderboard accessible. 759: 760: No participant can access another participant's submission. 761: 762: --- 763: 764: # 28. Future Enhancements 765: 766: Sports API integration. 767: 768: AI-powered prediction explanations. 769: 770: Model metadata submissions. 771: 772: CSV export of leaderboard. 773: 774: Tournament archive. 775: 776: Historical competitions. 777: 778: Multi-tournament support. 779: 780: --- 781: 782: # 29. AI Coding Constraints 783: 784: Generate production-quality code. 785: 786: Use strict TypeScript. 787: 788: Use server actions where appropriate. 789: 790: Avoid mock data. 791: 792: Implement database migrations. 793: 794: Implement proper error handling. 795: 796: Implement loading states. 797: 798: Implement optimistic updates. 799: 800: Use reusable components. 801: 802: Follow clean architecture principles. 803: 804: Avoid unnecessary complexity. 805: 806: Target deployment on Vercel. 807: 808: Platform expected scale: 809: 810: <500 participants. 811: 812: Prioritize maintainability and reliability over scalability. 1: # QA_TEST_PLAN.md 2: 3: # FIFA World Cup 2026 ML Prediction Challenge 4: 5: ## End-to-End Validation Plan 6: 7: Version: 1.0 8: 9: --- 10: 11: # Objective 12: 13: Before marking the project as complete, the agent must verify that every page, workflow, API endpoint, database interaction, authentication flow, scoring calculation, and leaderboard update works correctly. 14: 15: The agent must not assume functionality works. 16: 17: The agent must test and verify. 18: 19: --- 20: 21: # Global Rule 22: 23: For every feature: 24: 25: 1. Verify UI renders correctly 26: 2. Verify data loads correctly 27: 3. Verify database updates correctly 28: 4. Verify authorization rules 29: 5. Verify error handling 30: 6. Verify loading states 31: 7. SCORING_ENGINE_SPEC.md 2: 3: # FIFA World Cup 2026 ML Prediction Challenge 4: 5: ## Scoring Engine Specification 6: 7: Version: 1.0 8: 9: --- 10: 11: # Purpose 12: 13: This document defines the complete scoring logic for evaluating participant predictions against actual FIFA World Cup match results. 14: 15: The scoring engine is the source of truth for leaderboard rankings. 16: 17: All scoring calculations must follow this specification exactly. 18: 19: --- 20: 21: # Design Principles 22: 23: The scoring system must: 24: 25: * Reward accuracy 26: * Reward near-accurate predictions 27: * Reward difficult predictions 28: * Encourage calibrated confidence 29: * Be transparent 30: * Be configurable 31: 32: All point values must be loaded from the database. 33: 34: Default values are defined below. 35: 36: --- 37: 38: # Scoring Architecture 39: 40: Final Match Score 41: 42: = 43: 44: Outcome Score 45: 46: * 47: 48: Scoreline Score 49: 50: * 51: 52: Scorer Score 53: 54: * 55: 56: Statistics Score 57: 58: * 59: 60: Penalty Score 61: 62: * 63: 64: Confidence Score 65: 66: All multiplied by Match Stage Multiplier. 67: 68: Tournament Champion score is added separately. 69: 70: --- 71: 72: # Stage Multipliers 73: 74: Applied after match score calculation. 75: 76: | Stage | Multiplier | 77: | ------------- | ---------- | 78: | Group Stage | 1.0 | 79: | Round of 32 | 1.2 | 80: | Round of 16 | 1.5 | 81: | Quarter Final | 2.0 | 82: | Semi Final | 3.0 | 83: | Third Place | 2.5 | 84: | Final | 5.0 | 85: 86: Formula: 87: 88: Final Match Score 89: 90: = 91: 92: Base Match Score × Stage Multiplier 93: 94: --- 95: 96: # Outcome Scoring 97: 98: ## Correct Winner 99: 100: Condition: 101: 102: Predicted winner equals actual winner. 103: 104: Default Points: 105: 106: 20 107: 108: --- 109: 110: ## Correct Draw Prediction 111: 112: Condition: 113: 114: Predicted draw and actual draw after regulation time. 115: 116: Default Points: 117: 118: 20 119: 120: --- 121: 122: # Scoreline Scoring 123: 124: ## Exact Scoreline 125: 126: Example: 127: 128: Prediction: 129: 130: Brazil 3-1 Spain 131: 132: Actual: 133: 134: Brazil 3-1 Spain 135: 136: Points: 137: 138: 40 139: 140: --- 141: 142: ## Correct Goal Difference 143: 144: Example: 145: 146: Prediction: 147: 148: Brazil 2-0 Spain 149: 150: Actual: 151: 152: Brazil 3-1 Spain 153: 154: Goal Difference: 155: 156: Both = +2 157: 158: Points: 159: 160: 15 161: 162: --- 163: 164: ## One Team Goal Count Correct 165: 166: Example: 167: 168: Prediction: 169: 170: Brazil 3-1 Spain 171: 172: Actual: 173: 174: Brazil 3-2 Spain 175: 176: Brazil score predicted correctly. 177: 178: Points: 179: 180: 10 181: 182: Award once per match. 183: 184: Maximum: 185: 186: 10 187: 188: --- 189: 190: # Scorer Scoring 191: 192: Goal scorers stored as: 193: 194: Mbappe:2;Dembele:1 195: 196: Meaning: 197: 198: Mbappe scored 2 goals. 199: 200: Dembele scored 1 goal. 201: 202: --- 203: 204: ## Correct Scorer 205: 206: For each correctly predicted scorer. 207: 208: Default: 209: 210: 10 points 211: 212: Example: 213: 214: Actual: 215: 216: Mbappe 217: Dembele 218: 219: Prediction: 220: 221: Mbappe 222: Rodrygo 223: 224: Points: 225: 226: 10 227: 228: --- 229: 230: ## Correct Goal Count By Scorer 231: 232: Condition: 233: 234: Scorer predicted AND goal count predicted correctly. 235: 236: Example: 237: 238: Prediction: 239: 240: Mbappe:2 241: 242: Actual: 243: 244: Mbappe:2 245: 246: Points: 247: 248: 10 249: 250: Per scorer. 251: 252: --- 253: 254: ## Exact Scorer List 255: 256: Condition: 257: 258: All scorers predicted correctly. 259: 260: No missing scorers. 261: 262: No extra scorers. 263: 264: Points: 265: 266: 25 267: 268: --- 269: 270: # First Goal Scorer 271: 272: Condition: 273: 274: Predicted first goal scorer equals actual first goal scorer. 275: 276: Default: 277: 278: 15 points 279: 280: --- 281: 282: # Statistics Scoring 283: 284: Statistics use tolerance ranges. 285: 286: --- 287: 288: ## Possession Accuracy 289: 290: Tolerance: 291: 292: ±5% 293: 294: Example: 295: 296: Actual: 297: 298: 58% 299: 300: Prediction: 301: 302: 61% 303: 304: Difference: 305: 306: 3% 307: 308: Points: 309: 310: 10 311: 312: --- 313: 314: ## Shots On Target Accuracy 315: 316: Tolerance: 317: 318: ±2 319: 320: Points: 321: 322: 10 323: 324: --- 325: 326: ## Expected Goals (xG) 327: 328: Tolerance: 329: 330: ±0.5 331: 332: Points: 333: 334: 10 335: 336: --- 337: 338: ## Yellow Cards 339: 340: Tolerance: 341: 342: ±1 343: 344: Points: 345: 346: 5 347: 348: --- 349: 350: ## Red Cards 351: 352: Exact Match Required. 353: 354: Points: 355: 356: 10 357: 358: --- 359: 360: # Penalty Shootout Scoring 361: 362: Only applies if penalties occur. 363: 364: --- 365: 366: ## Correct Shootout Winner 367: 368: Points: 369: 370: 20 371: 372: --- 373: 374: ## Exact Penalty Score 375: 376: Example: 377: 378: Prediction: 379: 380: 4-2 381: 382: Actual: 383: 384: 4-2 385: 386: Points: 387: 388: 30 389: 390: --- 391: 392: # Confidence Scoring 393: 394: Purpose: 395: 396: Reward calibrated predictions. 397: 398: --- 399: 400: ## Confidence Bonus 401: 402: Condition: 403: 404: Winner prediction correct 405: 406: AND 407: 408: Confidence > 80% 409: 410: Points: 411: 412: +10 413: 414: --- 415: 416: ## Confidence Penalty 417: 418: Condition: 419: 420: Winner prediction incorrect 421: 422: AND 423: 424: Confidence > 80% 425: 426: Points: 427: 428: -10 429: 430: --- 431: 432: ## Confidence Neutral Zone 433: 434: Confidence ≤ 80% 435: 436: No bonus 437: 438: No penalty 439: 440: --- 441: 442: # Own Goal Handling 443: 444: Own goals do not count as player scorer predictions. 445: 446: Own goals only affect: 447: 448: Winner 449: 450: Scoreline 451: 452: Statistics 453: 454: --- 455: 456: # Extra Time Handling 457: 458: Winner evaluation uses official match winner. 459: 460: Example: 461: 462: 1-1 after regulation 463: 464: 2-1 after extra time 465: 466: Winner = Team A 467: 468: Scoring should use official result. 469: 470: --- 471: 472: # Penalty Handling 473: 474: Example: 475: 476: 1-1 after extra time 477: 478: 4-3 penalties 479: 480: Winner = Team A 481: 482: Scoreline remains: 483: 484: 1-1 485: 486: Penalty scoring evaluated separately. 487: 488: --- 489: 490: # Champion Prediction 491: 492: Evaluated after tournament completion. 493: 494: --- 495: 496: ## Correct Champion 497: 498: Condition: 499: 500: Predicted champion equals actual champion. 501: 502: Points: 503: 504: 250 505: 506: --- 507: 508: # Match Maximum Score 509: 510: System must calculate dynamically. 511: 512: Formula: 513: 514: Sum of enabled scoring rules. 515: 516: Multiplied by stage multiplier. 517: 518: --- 519: 520: # Accuracy Percentage 521: 522: Purpose: 523: 524: Leaderboard normalization. 525: 526: Formula: 527: 528: Accuracy % 529: 530: = 531: 532: Earned Points 533: 534: / 535: 536: Maximum Available Points 537: 538: × 539: 540: 100 541: 542: Example: 543: 544: 450 545: 546: / 547: 548: 600 549: 550: × 551: 552: 100 553: 554: = 555: 556: 75% 557: 558: --- 559: 560: # Leaderboard Ranking Logic 561: 562: Primary: 563: 564: Total Score DESC 565: 566: Secondary: 567: 568: Accuracy Percentage DESC 569: 570: Tertiary: 571: 572: Winner Accuracy DESC 573: 574: Fourth: 575: 576: Earlier Submission Timestamp ASC 577: 578: --- 579: 580: # Team Accuracy Metrics 581: 582: Track separately: 583: 584: Winner Accuracy % 585: 586: Scoreline Accuracy % 587: 588: Scorer Accuracy % 589: 590: Statistics Accuracy % 591: 592: Penalty Accuracy % 593: 594: Champion Accuracy % 595: 596: Confidence Calibration Score 597: 598: --- 599: 600: # Recalculation Triggers 601: 602: Recalculate scores when: 603: 604: Actual Result Updated 605: 606: Scoring Rule Updated 607: 608: Multiplier Updated 609: 610: Admin Recalculation Requested 611: 612: --- 613: 614: # Recalculation Workflow 615: 616: Load Predictions 617: 618: ↓ 619: 620: Load Actual Results 621: 622: ↓ 623: 624: Calculate Match Scores 625: 626: ↓ 627: 628: Apply Multipliers 629: 630: ↓ 631: 632: Calculate Champion Score 633: 634: ↓ 635: 636: Calculate Total Score 637: 638: ↓ 639: 640: Update Leaderboard 641: 642: ↓ 643: 644: Update Analytics 645: 646: ↓ 647: 648: Broadcast Realtime Events 649: 650: --- 651: 652: # Analytics Metrics 653: 654: Compute: 655: 656: Most Accurate Team 657: 658: Highest Confidence Team 659: 660: Best Scorer Predictor 661: 662: Best Outcome Predictor 663: 664: Hardest Match 665: 666: Most Predictable Match 667: 668: Average Accuracy 669: 670: Champion Prediction Distribution 671: 672: --- 673: 674: # Database Requirements 675: 676: All scoring rules stored in: 677: 678: scoring_rules 679: 680: table. 681: 682: No hardcoded values. 683: 684: All calculations must read values from database. 685: 686: --- 687: 688: # Edge Cases 689: 690: Match Cancelled 691: 692: Score = 0 693: 694: No points awarded. 695: 696: --- 697: 698: Result Corrected 699: 700: Automatically trigger full recalculation. 701: 702: --- 703: 704: Scoring Rule Changed 705: 706: Automatically trigger full recalculation. 707: 708: --- 709: 710: Missing Actual Result 711: 712: Skip scoring until result exists. 713: 714: --- 715: 716: Submission Missing Match 717: 718: Submission rejected. 719: 720: --- 721: 722: Invalid Player Names 723: 724: Submission rejected. 725: 726: --- 727: 728: # Implementation Requirements 729: 730: Scoring engine must be: 731: 732: Pure 733: 734: Deterministic 735: 736: Idempotent 737: 738: Fully testable 739: 740: Independent from UI 741: 742: Reusable 743: 744: No side effects during score calculation. 745: 746: Scoring engine should return: 747: 748: Match Score 749: 750: Score Breakdown 751: 752: Category Breakdown 753: 754: Multiplier Applied 755: 756: Final Score 757: 758: Leaderboard Contribution 759: 760: For every evaluated match. 1: # FIFA World Cup 2026 ML Prediction Challenge 2: 3: # Technical Design Document (TDD) 4: 5: **Version:** 1.0 6: 7: --- 8: 9: # 1. 1.5" fill="#666"/></svg> 1: # µFifa '26 — ML Prediction Challenge Platform 🏆 2: 3: Welcome to the **µFifa '26** repository! This is a comprehensive, enterprise-grade web application built to host machine learning prediction challenges for football tournaments. It allows participants to download templates, run their ML models locally, upload their predictions via CSV, and compete on a global real-time leaderboard. 4: 5: ![Dashboard Preview](https://mufifa-gules.vercel.app/favicon.ico) 6: 7: ## 🌟 Key Features 8: 9: * **Robust CSV Pipeline:** Drag-and-drop CSV uploads parsed with PapaParse and strictly validated against complex tournament constraints (score formats, possession constraints, missing matches, and fuzzy name matching) using Zod. 10: * **Deterministic Scoring Engine:** A purely functional scoring engine that calculates points across 19 separate rules (Outcome, Scoreline, Scorers, Stats, Penalties, Confidence, and Champions). 11: * **Real-Time Global Leaderboard:** Automatically updates rankings and breaks ties based on a hierarchy of scoring categories without requiring page refreshes. 12: * **Comprehensive Admin Dashboard:** Protected routes allowing tournament organizers to create matches, enter live real-world results, adjust rule point values, and globally recalculate all participant scores. 13: * **Analytics & Visualizations:** Rich, interactive charts built with Recharts, featuring global accuracy distributions, champion pick breakdowns, and 1v1 team comparison radar charts. 14: * **Secure by Design:** Strict Row-Level Security (RLS) in PostgreSQL ensures participants only access their own submissions, while Supabase Storage acts as a secure audit trail for original CSV files. 15: 16: ## 🛠 Tech Stack 17: 18: * **Framework:** [Next.js 15](https://nextjs.org/) (App Router, Server Actions, Server Components) 19: * **Styling:** [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/) & [shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com/) 20: * **Database & Auth:** [Supabase](https://supabase.com/) (PostgreSQL, Supabase Auth, Storage) 21: * **Validation:** [Zod](https://zod.dev/) 22: * **Data Parsing:** [PapaParse](https://www.papaparse.com/) 23: * **Visualizations:** [Recharts](https://recharts.org/) 24: * **Tables:** [TanStack Table v8](https://tanstack.com/table/v8) 25: 26: --- 27: 28: ## 🚀 Local Development Setup 29: 30: To run this application locally, you will need **Node.js 20+** and the **Supabase CLI** installed. 31: 32: ### 1. Clone & Install Dependencies 33: ```bash 34: git clone https://github.com/your-username/Mufifa.git 35: cd Mufifa 36: npm install 37: ``` 38: 39: ### 2. Start the Local Database 40: We use Supabase's local development environment to spin up Postgres and run our migrations. 41: ```bash 42: npx supabase start 43: ``` 44: *Note: This command will automatically apply all the SQL migrations found in the `supabase/migrations` folder and seed the default scoring rules.* 45: 46: ### 3. Environment Variables 47: Copy the template environment file: 48: ```bash 49: cp .env.example .env.local 50: ``` 51: Fill in the `.env.local` file with the API credentials provided in your terminal after running `supabase start`. Ensure you also set an `ADMIN_EMAIL` to auto-grant admin privileges to your first account! 52: 53: ### 4. Run the Application 54: ```bash 55: npm run dev 56: ``` 57: Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) in your browser. 58: 59: --- 60: 61: ## ☁️ Deployment 62: 63: This application is fully optimized for **Vercel** deployment. 64: 65: 1. Create a new project in Supabase cloud. 66: 2. Link your Vercel project to your GitHub repository. 67: 3. Push your database migrations to your production Supabase project: 68: ```bash 69: npx supabase link --project-ref YOUR_PROJECT_ID 70: npx supabase db push 71: ``` 72: 4. Copy your Supabase URL, Anon Key, and Service Role Key into your Vercel Environment Variables. 73: 5. Hit **Deploy**. 74: 75: --- 76: 77: ## 🔒 Post-Deployment Security 78: 79: By default, Supabase's test SMTP server only allows 3 emails per hour. For hackathons or testing environments without a custom SMTP provider, it is highly recommended to **disable Email Confirmations** in your Supabase Auth settings so participants can register seamlessly without hitting rate limits. 80: 81: --- 82: 83: *Built with precision for the ultimate football prediction challenge.* 1: /* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any */ 2: 'use 18: await supabase.from('audit_logs').insert({ 19: actor_id: actorId, 20: action, 21: entity_type: entityType, 22: entity_id: entityId, 23: payload, 24: created_at: new Date().toISOString() 25: }) 26: false }) 39: .range(from, to) 40: 41: if (error) { 42: console.error('Error fetching logs:', error) 43: return { rows: [], totalCount: 0 } 44: } 45: 46: return { rows: data || [], totalCount: count || 0 } 47: } 1: 'use server' 2: 3: import { createAdminClient } from '@/lib/supabase/admin' 4: import { logAuditEvent } from './audit' 5: import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache' 6: import { requireAdmin() 11: const supabase = createAdminClient() 12: 13: // Extract all data from formData 14: const winner = formData.get('winner') as string 15: const homeScore = parseInt(formData.get('home_score') as string) 16: const awayScore = parseInt(formData.get('away_score') as string) 17: const possessionHome = parseFloat(formData.get('possession_home') as string) 18: const possessionAway = parseFloat(formData.get('possession_away') as string) 19: const shotsHome = parseInt(formData.get('shots_home') as string) 20: const shotsAway = parseInt(formData.get('shots_away') as string) 21: const xgHome = parseFloat(formData.get('xg_home') as string) 22: const xgAway = parseFloat(formData.get('xg_away') as string) 23: const yellowHome = parseInt(formData.get('yellow_home') as string) 24: const yellowAway = parseInt(formData.get('yellow_away') as string) 25: const redHome = parseInt(formData.get('red_home') as string) 26: const redAway = parseInt(formData.get('red_away') as string) 27: 28: const penaltyHomeRaw = formData.get('penalty_home') 29: const penaltyHome = penaltyHomeRaw ? parseInt(penaltyHomeRaw as string) : null 30: const penaltyAwayRaw = formData.get('penalty_away') 31: const penaltyAway = penaltyAwayRaw ? parseInt(penaltyAwayRaw as string) : null 32: 33: // Process Goal Scorers 34: const goalScorersRaw = formData.get('goal_scorers') as string 35: let goalScorers: { name: string; goals: number }[] = [] 36: if (goalScorersRaw) { 37: // Format: Player A:2;Player B:1 38: const parts = goalScorersRaw.split(';').filter(p => p.trim() !== '') 39: goalScorers = parts.map(p => { 40: const [name, goals] = p.split(':') 41: return { name: name.trim(), goals: parseInt(goals.trim()) } 42: }) 43: } 44: 45: const firstGoalScorerRaw = formData.get('first_goal_scorer') as string 46: const firstGoalScorer = firstGoalScorerRaw ? firstGoalScorerRaw.trim() : null 47: 48: const { error } = await supabase.from('actual_results').upsert({ 49: match_id: matchId, 50: winner, 51: home_score: homeScore, 52: away_score: awayScore, 53: possession_home: possessionHome, 54: possession_away: possessionAway, 55: shots_home: shotsHome, 56: shots_away: shotsAway, 57: xg_home: xgHome, 58: xg_away: xgAway, 59: yellow_home: yellowHome, 60: yellow_away: yellowAway, 61: red_home: redHome, 62: red_away: redAway, 63: penalty_home: penaltyHome, 64: penalty_away: penaltyAway, 65: goal_scorers: goalScorers, 66: first_goal_scorer: firstGoalScorer, 67: updated_by: adminUser.id, 68: updated_at: new Date().toISOString() 69: }, { onConflict: 'match_id' }) 70: 71: if (error) return { error: error.message } 72: 73: // Update Match Status to completed 74: await supabase.from('matches').update({ status: 'completed' }).eq('id', matchId) 75: 76: // Trigger Recalculation 77: await recalculateForMatch(matchId) 78: 79: await logAuditEvent('enter_match_result', 'actual_results', matchId) 80: 81: revalidatePath('/admin/results') 82: revalidatePath('/admin/matches') 83: revalidatePath('/leaderboard') 84: revalidatePath('/dashboard') 85: 86: return { success: true } 87: } 1: 'use server' 2: 3: import { createAdminClient } from '@/lib/supabase/admin' 4: import { logAuditEvent } from './audit' 5: import { recalculateAll } from '@/lib/scoring/calculator' 6: import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache' 7: import { requireAdmin } from './require-admin' 8: 9: export async function getScoringRules() { 10: await requireAdmin() 11: const supabase = createAdminClient() 12: const { data, error } = await supabase.from('scoring_rules').select('*').order('id') 13: 14: if (error) { 15: console.error('Error fetching scoring rules:', error) 16: return [] 17: } 18: return data || [] 19: } 20: 21: export async function updateScoringRules(formData: FormData) { 22: await requireAdmin() 23: const supabase = createAdminClient() 24: 25: const rulesToUpdate = [] 26: 27: // Extract all inputs matching pattern rule_[id]_points 28: for (const [key, value] of formData.entries()) { 29: if (key.startsWith('rule_') && key.endsWith('_points')) { 30: const id = key.split('_')[1] 31: const points = parseInt(value as string) 32: const isEnabled = formData.get(`rule_${id}_enabled`) === 'on' 33: 34: rulesToUpdate.push({ 35: id, 36: points, 37: is_enabled: isEnabled, 38: updated_at: new Date().toISOString() 39: }) 40: } 41: } 42: 43: for (const rule of rulesToUpdate) { 44: await supabase 45: .from('scoring_rules') 46: .update({ points: rule.points, is_enabled: rule.is_enabled, updated_at: rule.updated_at }) 47: .eq('id', rule.id) 48: } 49: 50: await logAuditEvent('update_scoring_rules', 'scoring_rules', null, { updated_count: rulesToUpdate.length }) 51: 52: // Recalculate 53: await recalculateAll() 54: 55: revalidatePath('/admin/scoring') 56: revalidatePath('/leaderboard') 57: } 1: 'use server' 2: 3: import { createAdminClient } from '@/lib/supabase/admin' 4: import { logAuditEvent } from './audit' 5: import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache' 6: import { requireAdmin } from './require-admin' 7: 8: export async function getUsers(page: number = 1, pageSize: number = 50, search: string = '') { 9: await requireAdmin() 10: const supabase = createAdminClient() 11: 12: let query = supabase 13: .from('profiles') 14: .select(` 15: *, 16: teams(id, team_name, submission_locked, submissions(id, is_valid)) 17: `, { count: 'exact' }) 18: .order('created_at', { ascending: false }) 19: 20: if (search) { 21: query = query.ilike('email', `%${search}%`) 22: } 23: 24: const from = (page - 1) * pageSize 25: const to = from + pageSize - 1 26: 27: const { data, count, error } = await query.range(from, to) 28: 29: if (error) { 30: console.error('Error fetching users:', error) 31: return { rows: [], totalCount: 0 } 32: } 33: 34: return { rows: data || [], totalCount: count || 0 } 35: } 36: 37: export async function toggleUserStatus(userId: string, currentStatus: boolean) { 38: await requireAdmin() 39: const supabase = createAdminClient() 40: 41: const { error } = await supabase 42: .from('profiles') 43: .update({ is_active: !currentStatus }) 44: .eq('id', userId) 45: 46: if (error) { 47: console.error(error.message) 48: return 49: } 50: 51: await logAuditEvent('toggle_user_status', 'profiles', userId, { new_status: !currentStatus }) 52: revalidatePath('/admin/users') 53: } 1: /* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any */ 2: 'use server' 3: 4: import { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase/server' 5: 6: export async function getTournamentAnalytics() { 7: const supabase = await createClient() 8: 9: // In a full production scenario, these would ideally be materialized views or pre-aggregated 10: // via triggers into the `analytics_cache` table to avoid heavy scans on every page load. 11: // We'll perform direct queries here for simplicity given the dataset size. 12: 13: const { count: totalTeams } = await supabase.from('teams').select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true }) 14: 15: // Get all leaderboard entries 16: const { data: leaderboard } = await supabase.from('leaderboard').select('*') 17: 18: // Calculate average accuracy 19: let avgAccuracy = 0 20: let bestTeam = null 21: 22: if (leaderboard && leaderboard.length > 0) { 23: const totalAccuracy = leaderboard.reduce((sum, entry: any) => sum + (Number(entry.accuracy_percentage) || 0), 0) 24: avgAccuracy = totalAccuracy / leaderboard.length 25: 26: // Best team is the one with highest score 27: const topEntry: any = [...leaderboard].sort((a: any, b: any) => Number(b.total_score) - Number(a.total_score))[0] 28: 29: if (topEntry) { 30: const { data: teamData } = await supabase.from('teams').select('team_name').eq('id', topEntry.team_id).single() 31: bestTeam = teamData?.team_name 32: } 33: } 34: 35: // Champion Distribution 36: const { data: champPreds } = await supabase.from('champion_predictions').select('champion') 37: const championDist: Record<string, number> = {} 38: 39: if (champPreds) { 40: champPreds.forEach((pred: any) => { 41: const champ = pred.champion 42: championDist[champ] = (championDist[champ] || 0) + 1 43: }) 44: } 45: 46: // Convert to array for Recharts 47: const championChartData = Object.entries(championDist) 48: .map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value })) 49: .sort((a, b) => b.value - a.value) 50: .slice(0, 10) // Top 10 predicted champions 51: 52: // Accuracy Distribution Histogram Data 53: const bins = [ 54: { name: '0-10%', count: 0 }, 55: { name: '10-20%', count: 0 }, 56: { name: '20-30%', count: 0 }, 57: { name: '30-40%', count: 0 }, 58: { name: '40-50%', count: 0 }, 59: { name: '50-60%', count: 0 }, 60: { name: '60-70%', count: 0 }, 61: { name: '70-80%', count: 0 }, 62: { name: '80-90%', count: 0 }, 63: { name: '90-100%', count: 0 } 64: ] 65: 66: if (leaderboard) { 67: leaderboard.forEach((entry: any) => { 68: const acc = Number(entry.accuracy_percentage) || 0 69: const index = Math.min(Math.floor(acc / 10), 9) // cap at 9 for 100% 70: bins[index].count++ 71: }) 72: } 73: 74: return { 75: totalTeams: totalTeams || 0, 76: avgAccuracy, 77: bestTeam, 78: championChartData, 79: accuracyDistribution: bins 80: } 81: } 82: 83: export async function getComparisonData(teamAId: string, teamBId: string) { 84: const supabase = await createClient() 85: 86: if (!teamAId || !teamBId) return null 87: 88: const { data: teams } = await supabase 89: .from('leaderboard') 90: .select('*, teams(team_name)') 91: .in('team_id', [teamAId, teamBId]) 92: 93: if (!teams || teams.length !== 2) return null 94: 95: const t1: any = teams[0] 96: const t2: any = teams[1] 97: 98: // Structure for radar chart 99: const categories = [ 100: { key: 'winner_score', label: 'Outcome' }, 101: { key: 'scoreline_score', label: 'Scoreline' }, 102: { key: 'scorer_score', label: 'Scorers' }, 103: { key: 47: team_id: team.id, 48: file_path: 'failed_upload', 49: file_name: file.name, 50: is_valid: false, 51: validation_errors: validationResult.errors, 52: }) 53: return { validationResult } 54: } 55: 70: const { data: allMatches } = await supabase.from('matches').select('id, match_code') 71: const matchMap = new Map((allMatches || []).map(m => [m.match_code, m.id])) 72: 73: const predictionInserts p.first_goal_scorer, 85: possession_home: p.possession_home, 86: possession_away: p.possession_away, 87: shots_home: p.shots_home, 88: shots_away: p.shots_away, 89: xg_home: p.xg_home, 90: xg_away: p.xg_away, 91: yellow_home: p.yellow_home, 92: yellow_away: p.yellow_away, 93: red_home: p.red_home, 94: red_away: p.red_away, 95: confidence: p.confidence 96: })) 97: 98: const { error: predictionError } = await supabase.from('predictions').insert(predictionInserts) 99: if (predictionError) return { error: 'Failed to save predictions: ' + predictionError.message } 100: 101: // Insert champion 102: if (validationResult.champion) { 103: await supabase.from('champion_predictions').insert({ 104: team_id: team.id, 105: champion: validationResult.champion 106: }) 107: } 108: 109: // Record successful submission 110: await supabase.from('submissions').insert({ 111: team_id: team.id, 112: file_path: filePath, 113: file_name: file.name, 114: is_valid: true, 115: locked_at: new Date().toISOString() 116: }) 117: 118: // Lock team 119: await supabase.from('teams').update({ submission_locked: true }).eq('id', team.id) 120: 121: // Setup initial leaderboard entry 122: await supabase.from('leaderboard').insert({ team_id: team.id }) 123: 124: // Log audit 125: await supabase.from('audit_logs').insert({ 126: actor_id: user.id, 127: action: 'upload_submission', 128: entity_type: 'team', 129: entity_id: team.id 130: }) 131: 132: revalidatePath('/dashboard') 133: revalidatePath('/submit') 134: 135: return { success: true } 136: } 137: 138: export async function changes on the leaderboard table 15: const channel = supabase 16: .channel('leaderboard_changes') 17: .on( 18: 'postgres_changes', 19: { 20: event: '*', // listen to INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE 21: schema: 'public', 22: table: 'leaderboard' 23: }, 24: (payload) => { 25: setIsUpdating(true) 26: 27: // Refresh the current route to fetch new data 28: router.refresh() 29: 30: // Optional: Show a subtle toast that data was refreshed 31: toast.success('Leaderboard updated', { 32: description: 'New match results have been processed.', 33: duration: 3000, 34: position: 'top-center' 35: }) 36: 37: setTimeout(() => setIsUpdating(false), 1000) 38: } 39: ) 40: .subscribe() 41: 42: return () => { 43: supabase.removeChannel(channel) 44: } 45: }, [supabase, router]) 46: 47: return { isUpdating } 48: 70: xg: 0.5, 71: yellowCards: 1, 72: } 73: 74: export const CONFIDENCE_MIN = 0 75: export const CONFIDENCE_MAX = 100 1: // Simple Levenshtein distance implementation 2: function levenshteinDistance(a: string, b: string): number { 3: if (a.length === 0) return b.length 4: if (b.length === 0) return a.length 5: 6: const matrix = Array(b.length + 1).fill(null).map(() => Array(a.length + 1).fill(null)) 7: 8: for (let i = 0; i <= a.length; i += 1) { 9: matrix[0][i] = i 10: } 11: 12: for (let j = 0; j <= b.length; j += 1) { 13: matrix[j][0] = j 14: } 15: 16: for (let j = 1; j <= b.length; j += 1) { 17: for (let i = 1; i <= a.length; i += 1) { 18: const indicator = a[i - 1] === b[j - 1] ? 0 : 1 19: matrix[j][i] = Math.min( 20: matrix[j][i - 1] + 1, // deletion 21: matrix[j - 1][i] + 1, // insertion 22: matrix[j - 1][i - 1] + indicator // substitution 23: ) 24: } 25: } 26: 27: return matrix[b.length][a.length] 28: } 29: 30: export function normalizePlayerName( 31: inputName: string, 32: knownPlayers: string[], 33: threshold: number = 3 34: ): string | null { 35: if (!inputName) return null 36: 37: const normalizedInput = inputName.toLowerCase().trim() 38: 39: // 1. Exact match check 40: const exactMatch = knownPlayers.find(p => p.toLowerCase() === normalizedInput) 41: if (exactMatch) return exactMatch 42: 43: // 2. Fuzzy match finding lowest distance 44: let bestMatch: string | null = null 45: let minDistance = Infinity 46: 47: for (const player of knownPlayers) { 48: const normalizedPlayer = player.toLowerCase() 49: 50: // Quick inclusion check (e.g., "Mbappe" in "Kylian Mbappe") 51: if (normalizedPlayer.includes(normalizedInput) || normalizedInput.includes(normalizedPlayer)) { 52: return player // Return early on strong partial match 53: } 54: 55: const distance = levenshteinDistance(normalizedInput, normalizedPlayer) 56: if (distance < minDistance) { 57: minDistance = distance 58: bestMatch = player 59: } 60: } 61: 62: // Only return if within threshold 63: return minDistance <= threshold ? bestMatch : null 64: } 1: import Papa from 'papaparse' 2: import { CsvRow, GoalScorer } function calculateOutcomeScore(prediction: Prediction, actual: Actual, rules: Record<string, ScoringRule>) { 24: const predictedWinner = normalizeOutcome(prediction.winner, actual) 25: const } 30: if (actualWinner !== 'draw' && predictedWinner === actualWinner) { 31: return { points: rules['correct_winner']?.points || 0, type: 'winner' } 32: } 33: return { points: 0, type: 'none' } 34: } actualDiff) { 48: return rules['correct_goal_difference']?.points || 0 49: } 50: 51: if (prediction.home_score === actual.home_score || prediction.away_score === actual.away_score) { 52: return Prediction, actual: Actual, rules: Record<string, ScoringRule>) { 141: if (actual.penalty_home === null && actual.penalty_away === null) return 0 142: let points = 0 143: 144: // Penalty winner 145: const actualPenWinner = (actual.penalty_home || 0) > (actual.penalty_away || 0) ? 'home' : 'away' 146: const predPenWinner = (prediction.penalty_home || 0) > (prediction.penalty_away || 0) ? 'home' : 147: ((prediction.penalty_away || 0) > (prediction.penalty_home || 0) ? 'away' : null) 148: 149: if (predPenWinner === actualPenWinner) { 150: points += rules['penalty_winner']?.points || 0 151: } 152: 153: if (prediction.penalty_home === actual.penalty_home && prediction.penalty_away === actual.penalty_away) { 154: points += rules['penalty_score']?.points || 0 155: } 156: 157: return points 158: } 159: 160: export function calculateConfidenceScore(prediction: Prediction, actual: Actual, rules: Record<string, ScoringRule>) { 161: if (prediction.confidence === null || prediction.confidence <= 80) return 0 162: 163: const predictedWinner = normalizeOutcome(prediction.winner, actual) 164: const actualWinner = normalizeOutcome(actual.winner, actual) 165: const isWinnerCorrect = (actualWinner !== 'draw' && predictedWinner === actualWinner) || 166: (actualWinner === 'draw' && predictedWinner === 'draw') 167: 168: if (isWinnerCorrect) { 169: return rules['confidence_bonus']?.points || 0 170: } else { 171: return rules['confidence_penalty']?.points || 0 172: } 173: } 174: 175: export function calculateMaxPossibleScore(rules: Record<string, ScoringRule>, multiplier: number, hasPenalties: boolean): number { 176: let max = 0 177: 178: // Best outcome 179: max += Math.max(rules['correct_winner']?.points || 0, rules['correct_draw']?.points || 0) 180: // Best scoreline 181: max += rules['exact_scoreline']?.points || 0 182: // Best scorer (assumes 1 scorer for max, simplified max possible calculation) 183: max += (rules['correct_scorer']?.points || 0) + (rules['correct_goal_count']?.points || 0) + (rules['exact_scorer_list']?.points || 0) + (rules['first_goal_scorer']?.points || 0) 184: // Best stats 185: max += (rules['possession_accuracy']?.points || 0) + (rules['shots_accuracy']?.points || 0) + (rules['xg_accuracy']?.points || 0) + (rules['yellow_cards_accuracy']?.points || 0) + (rules['red_cards_exact']?.points || 0) 186: // Penalties 187: if (hasPenalties) { 188: max += (rules['penalty_winner']?.points || 0) + (rules['penalty_score']?.points || 0) 189: } 190: // Confidence 191: max += rules['confidence_bonus']?.points || 0 192: 193: return max * multiplier 194: } 195: 196: export function calculateMatchScore(prediction: Prediction, actual: Actual, rules: Record<string, ScoringRule>, multiplier: number): MatchScoreResult { 197: const outcome = calculateOutcomeScore(prediction, actual, rules).points 198: const scoreline = calculateScorelineScore(prediction, actual, rules) 199: const scorer = calculateScorerScore(prediction, actual, rules) 200: const stats = calculateStatsScore(prediction, actual, rules) 201: const penalty = calculatePenaltyScore(prediction, actual, rules) 202: const confidence = calculateConfidenceScore(prediction, actual, rules) 203: 204: const unmultipliedTotal = outcome + scoreline + scorer + stats + penalty + confidence 205: const multipliedTotal = unmultipliedTotal * multiplier 206: const hasPenalties = actual.penalty_home !== null || actual.penalty_away !== null 207: const maxPossible = calculateMaxPossibleScore(rules, multiplier, hasPenalties) 208: 209: return { 210: total: unmultipliedTotal, 211: multipliedTotal, 212: breakdown: { 213: outcome: outcome * multiplier, 214: scoreline: scoreline * multiplier, 215: scorer: scorer * multiplier, 216: stats: stats * multiplier, 217: penalty: penalty * multiplier, 218: confidence: confidence * multiplier 219: }, 220: multiplier, 221: maxPossible 222: } 223: } 224: 225: export function calculateChampionScore(predicted: string, actual: string, rules: Record<string, ScoringRule>) { 226: if (!predicted || !actual) return 0 227: if (normalizePlayerName(predicted, [actual], 3)) { 228: return rules['champion_prediction']?.points || 0 229: } 230: return 0 231: } 1: import { createAdminClient } from '../supabase/admin' 2: import { ScoringRule } from '@/types/scoring' 3: 4: export async function loadScoringRules(): Promise<Record<string, ScoringRule>> CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION is_admin() RETURNS BOOLEAN AS $$ 16: BEGIN 17: RETURN EXISTS ( 18: SELECT 1 FROM profiles 19: WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin' 20: ); 21: END; 22: $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER; 23: 24: -- profiles 25: CREATE POLICY "Users can read own profile" ON profiles 26: FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() = id); 27: CREATE POLICY "Admins can read all profiles" ON profiles 28: FOR SELECT USING (is_admin()); 29: 30: -- teams 31: CREATE POLICY "Users can read own team" ON teams 32: FOR SELECT USING (owner_id = auth.uid()); 33: CREATE POLICY "Users can insert own team" ON teams 34: FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (owner_id = auth.uid()); 35: CREATE POLICY "Admins can read all teams" ON teams 36: FOR SELECT USING (is_admin()); 37: 38: -- matches 39: CREATE POLICY "Matches are viewable by everyone" ON matches 40: FOR SELECT USING (true); 41: CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage matches" ON matches 42: FOR ALL USING (is_admin()); 43: 44: -- submissions 45: CREATE POLICY "Users can read own submissions" ON submissions 46: FOR SELECT USING ( 47: team_id IN (SELECT id FROM teams WHERE owner_id = auth.uid()) 48: ); 49: CREATE POLICY "Users can insert own (string_to_array(name, '/'))[1]) = auth.uid() 12: ); 13: 14: -- Allow admins to read all files 15: CREATE POLICY "Admins can read all prediction files" ON storage.objects 16: FOR SELECT USING ( 17: bucket_id = 'prediction-files' AND 18: EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM profiles WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin') 19: ); 1: -- Function to automatically create a profile when a new user signs up 2: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user() 3: RETURNS trigger AS $$ 4: BEGIN 5: INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, email, role) 6: VALUES ( 7: new.id, 8: new.email, 9: -- Simple check to see if email matches an admin email pattern or env config 10: -- For now, default to participant, first admin can be set manually or via edge function 11: 'participant' 12: ); 13: RETURN new; 14: END; 15: $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER; 16: 17: -- Trigger to call handle_new_user on signup 18: DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS on_auth_user_created ON auth.users; 19: CREATE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created 20: AFTER INSERT ON auth.users 21: FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE PROCEDURE public.handle_new_user(); 1: -- Add new fields to profiles table 2: ALTER TABLE public.profiles 3: ADD COLUMN full_name TEXT, 4: ADD COLUMN public.handle_new_user() 10: RETURNS trigger AS $$ 11: BEGIN 12: INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, email, role, full_name, phone_number, college, district) 13: VALUES ( 14: new.id, 15: new.email, 16: 'participant', 17: new.raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name', 18: new.raw_user_meta_data->>'phone_number', 19: new.raw_user_meta_data->>'college', 20: new.raw_user_meta_data->>'district' 21: ); 22: RETURN new; 23: END; 24: $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER; 1: { 2: "compilerOptions": { 3: "target": "ES2017", 4: "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"], 5: "allowJs": true, 6: "skipLibCheck": true, 7:
